title: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund
subtitle: The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.
caption: Global financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund rose from just under $30 billion in 2011 to approximately $80 billion in 2022, measured in current US dollars per year. The single line traces the total annual amount directed toward climate change mitigation and adaptation in developing countries. Between 2011 and 2012, the value remained flat near the $30 billion mark before climbing to roughly $41 billion in 2013. The trajectory continued upward to cross $50 billion in 2016, followed by a dip in 2017 that brought the total back below $50 billion. A recovery occurred in 2018, reaching a local peak around $56 billion, after which the line flattened through 2019 and 2020 at approximately $53 billion. From 2020 to 2022, the slope steepened significantly, with the value rising past $60 billion in 2021 and ending at the series maximum of $80 billion in 2022. The vertical axis spans from $0 to $80 billion in increments of $10 billion, while the horizontal axis covers the period from 2011 to 2022. The data shows a general upward trend over the twelve-year period, punctuated by periods of stagnation and a sharp acceleration in the final two years.
